title: torilis arvensis subsp. neglecta
intro: Torilis arvensis subsp. neglecta, commonly known as wild chervil or corn chervil, is a plant species belonging to the parsley family and is characterized by its fern-like leaves and small white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Torilis arvensis subsp. neglecta include its ability to support digestive health, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as a natural antacid. Torilis arvensis subsp. neglecta can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can be used fresh or dried in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
